library_preparation_preflight.summary: Spring Cloud Stream Kafka binder coverage normally requires an actual Kafka broker; upstream tests for this binder use Spring Kafka's embedded broker support rather than relying on the plain generated scaffold.

deterministic_setup.reason: The resolved binder depends on spring-kafka 4.0.2, and the upstream spring-cloud-stream-binder-kafka tests use spring-kafka-test's EmbeddedKafkaBroker/@EmbeddedKafka support to make binder send/receive and bootstrap coverage reachable.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: For integration-style tests, start an embedded Kafka broker with Spring Kafka test support and set the binder broker property from it, e.g. `spring.cloud.stream.kafka.binder.brokers=<embeddedKafka.getBrokersAsString()>` or use `${spring.embedded.kafka.brokers}` when `@EmbeddedKafka` publishes that property. Avoid assuming a broker is available at the default localhost:9092.
risks: Embedded Kafka is heavier than unit-only binder tests and may expose additional Native Image issues; local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
If the embedded broker approach fails under Native Image, the fallback may be a Docker-backed Kafka broker plus matching test setup, but current evidence points first to spring-kafka-test.
